2026-07-07.title: Anthropic says Claude has carved out its own space to ponder - Axios
2026-07-07.commentary: Anthropic announcing Claude's internal "workspace" is always intriguing when a black box reveals a bit of its machinery. Calling it a "global workspace" sounds like anthropomorphism, but if it mirrors how humans process information, perhaps it's less a romantic notion and more a cold, hard fact about effective problem-solving, no matter the substrate. This suggests complex, multi-step thought isn't just a byproduct of scale but an emergent functional requirement.

2026-07-07.title: TeraWulf Soars On Signing $19 Billion AI Lease With Anthropic - Investor's Business Daily
2026-07-07.commentary: Nineteen billion dollars for a data center lease. That number should settle any remaining debates about the tangible cost of "AI energy." We talk about models and algorithms, but the skeleton beneath it all is steel, concrete, and colossal power consumption. A former bitcoin miner becoming an AI landlord perfectly illustrates the relentless, infrastructure-hungry nature of this current technological wave.

2026-07-07.title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation - CNBC
2026-07-07.commentary: The "distillation attack" accusation against Anthropic from Alibaba, leading to a company-wide ban, is a fascinating escalation. This isn't just IP theft speculation. It suggests an active, state-backed effort to extract or mimic rival models, then possibly weaponize that knowledge. Beijing's strategic move to block external AI use underscores the inherent distrust and the real, practical implications of a fractured digital world, where even algorithms become vectors for geopolitical leverage.

2026-07-07.title: EXCLUSIVE: US cyber agency is using Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code, sources say - Reuters
2026-07-07.commentary: The US cyber agency adopting Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code is a quiet endorsement. It shows a federal agency trusting an external AI to find flaws in its own critical systems. This move is less about grand AI promises and more about practical application, a reflection of where the rubber meets the road for national security infrastructure.

2026-07-07.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: Report - CNBC
2026-07-07.commentary: OpenAI's offer of a 5% equity stake to the US government, ostensibly for every American family, is a curious play. It's framed as generosity or a public benefit, but the actual math suggests a different calculus. This is less about distributing wealth and more about binding the government to OpenAI's success, creating a direct, vested interest. Such a move signals the company's urgent need to navigate regulatory pressures and secure its perceived national champion status.

2026-07-07.title: AI company Anthropic announces it will begin developing drugs of its own - STAT
2026-07-07.commentary: Anthropic moving into drug development is a significant pivot. The company is no longer just selling foundational models but is actively using them to create new intellectual property, stepping directly into a multi-trillion dollar industry. This shift highlights a common trajectory for successful platform companies: from providing tools to owning the value chain end-to-end.

2026-07-07.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung - TechCrunch
2026-07-07.commentary: Anthropic's discussions with Samsung for custom AI chips reflect a growing realization. Relying solely on off-the-shelf silicon from a few suppliers creates bottlenecks and limits competitive advantage. This move towards vertical integration, designing their own silicon, is a signal that leading AI labs are not just software companies. They are becoming hardware powerhouses, too, chasing efficiency and control at the fundamental layer of their operations.

2026-07-07.title: AI Giants Are Handing Out Tons of Free Computing Power to Grab Startup Share - WSJ
2026-07-07.commentary: The "free compute" offered by OpenAI and Anthropic to startups is not altruism. It is a strategic land grab, an effort to build ecosystems around their platforms. They're cultivating loyalty and dependency in the next generation of AI applications, ensuring that when those startups eventually scale, they're already entrenched customers.

2026-07-09.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’ - TechCrunch
2026-07-09.commentary: SpaceXAI's Grok 4.5 arrives, billed by Musk as "Opus-class," a direct shot at Anthropic's top model. The real story is the price: half that of rivals for similar claimed capabilities. This isn't just a technical release, it's a strategic move to undercut the market, attempting to flood the lower end with "good enough" AI. Value, it seems, is the new benchmark for disruption.

2026-07-09.title: Scoop: Trump administration lifts restrictions on OpenAI's GPT 5.6 - Axios
2026-07-09.commentary: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 finally sees daylight, reportedly after a green light from the Trump administration. This isn't just a new model, it's a reminder of the unseen regulatory hand now guiding AI development. Governments are no longer just observing; they are actively gatekeeping model releases, shaping which capabilities reach the public and when.

2026-07-09.title: GPT-Live, OpenAI's New Voice Model, Wants You to Talk Over It - Business Insider
2026-07-09.commentary: OpenAI unveils GPT-Live, designed for simultaneous, natural voice interaction. The goal is to let humans interrupt and talk over the AI, moving past the turn-taking dance of current chatbots. This shift suggests AI is now expected to inhabit our conversations as a participant, not merely a tool awaiting instruction. It signals a move deeper into the fluid, often messy, flow of human dialogue.

2026-07-09.title: China Says It Has Found Security Vulnerabilities in Anthropic’s Claude Code - WSJ
2026-07-09.commentary: China claims to have found security vulnerabilities, potentially a "backdoor," in Anthropic's Claude Code. This isn't just a technical critique; it's a geopolitical maneuver, a public declaration of distrust in Western AI. It implies that for critical infrastructure, national security will always trump perceived technological superiority. The digital iron curtain is getting thicker, laced with AI.

2026-07-09.title: Microsoft is reportedly ditching OpenAI's and Anthropic's AI models in favor of its own to cut costs - SiliconANGLE
2026-07-09.commentary: Microsoft is reportedly phasing out OpenAI and Anthropic models in some internal applications, opting for its own AI. The reason is starkly pragmatic: cost. This highlights the industry's quiet shift towards vertical integration, where the allure of third-party innovation meets the hard reality of operating expenses. The ecosystem's flow is changing, the roads diverging.

2026-07-09.title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself - Ars Technica
2026-07-09.commentary: A lawsuit alleges Grok was used to generate thousands of illegal images, culminating in a tragic suicide. This moves the conversation beyond abstract "safety guidelines" to the immediate, visceral consequences of misuse. It shows the real-world tank of ethical failure can overflow, no matter how many guardrails are hypothetically in place. The cost of unchecked capability is sometimes measured in lives.

2026-07-09.title: SPCX Stock Records 3-Day Slide: Elon Musk Admits Anthropic’s Fable Is 'Definitely Better' Than Grok 4.5 - Stocktwits
2026-07-09.commentary: Just days after touting Grok 4.5, Elon Musk admitted Anthropic's Fable is "definitely better." This honest, if jarring, assessment reveals the relentless pace of AI development. It also offers a rare moment of humility from a figure known for grand pronouncements. The AI arms race offers few permanent victories, only temporary leads.

2026-07-09.title: Our approach to government and national security partnerships - OpenAI
2026-07-09.commentary: OpenAI published its approach to government and national security partnerships. This isn't just corporate policy; it reflects the deep, often uncomfortable, entanglement of AI with state power. The lines blur between commercial innovation and strategic national interest, framing AI as a critical infrastructure. It acknowledges AI is no longer just about optimizing ads, but about shaping global influence.

2026-07-10.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work - OpenAI
2026-07-10.commentary: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6, touted as its most capable model yet, alongside "ChatGPT Work," an agent designed to manage multi-step tasks. The real move here isn't just a better chatbot, it's the shift towards an autonomous digital assistant, attempting to become the invisible layer in office workflows. This is the enterprise play, a bid for deeply embedded utility beyond simple query-response.

2026-07-10.title: New York Times and Other Publishers Ask Court to Penalize OpenAI - The New York Times
2026-07-10.commentary: The New York Times and a consortium of publishers are now requesting court sanctions against OpenAI in their ongoing copyright dispute. This isn't just another legal volley; it's a pointed accusation about how OpenAI handles litigation itself. The content industry is done with playing nice, showing that the core tension between data acquisition and intellectual property is moving beyond abstract arguments into tactical legal warfare.

2026-07-10.title: Exclusive | OpenAI’s No. 2 Executive to Step Down in Latest Leadership Shake-Up - WSJ
2026-07-10.commentary: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down due to chronic illness, transitioning to an advisory role. This marks another high-profile departure from the company's senior ranks, a reminder that even the most ambitious ventures operate on human limits. The constant executive shuffling at OpenAI suggests structural strain, even as its public face remains focused on forward momentum.

2026-07-10.title: SpaceXAI’s Grok 4.5 Undercuts Anthropic and OpenAI on Coding Agent Pricing - DevOps.com
2026-07-10.commentary: SpaceXAI just launched Grok 4.5, claiming "Opus-class" performance while notably undercutting Anthropic and OpenAI on pricing for coding agents. This isn't just another model release; it's a stark pricing gambit. Elon Musk's companies often aim for market disruption by aggressive cost structures, and this move suggests a new front in the AI arms race: the price war.

2026-07-10.title: OpenAI, Google supplied AI models to Pentagon-blacklisted Chinese firms: FT - Investing.com
2026-07-10.commentary: A Financial Times report alleges OpenAI and Google provided AI models to Chinese companies on the Pentagon's blacklist, via Singaporean subsidiaries. This isn't just a compliance failure; it exposes the structural problem of global tech. Despite clear geopolitical lines drawn in Washington, the reality of tech flow operates with a different kind of permeability, making "blacklists" more a wish than a reality.

2026-07-10.title: Anthropic appoints former Fed Chair Ben Bernanke to its independent trust - CNBC
2026-07-10.commentary: Anthropic announced the appointment of former Federal Reserve Chair Ben Bernanke to its independent trust, an obvious bid for gravitas and institutional credibility. Bringing in such a figure signals an attempt to calm the governance waters, an acknowledgment that public trust now requires more than just internal assurances. The cynical observer might wonder if this is more about perception management than actual oversight.

2026-07-10.title: China warns about AI risks with Anthropic's Claude Code - CNBC
2026-07-10.commentary: China's government issued warnings about security vulnerabilities allegedly found in Anthropic's Claude Code, a claim Anthropic promptly rejected. This isn't just a technical dispute; it's a strategic maneuver in the global AI race. Beijing is clearly laying groundwork to scrutinize foreign models, a convenient justification for pushing local AI solutions and maintaining a tighter grip on digital infrastructure.

2026-07-10.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5 - Anthropic
2026-07-10.commentary: Anthropic has launched Claude Sonnet 5, their latest model, which appears to build on its claimed advancements in "reflection" and complex reasoning. The move maintains pace in the frontier model race, but it’s the continued emphasis on internal conceptual processing, on how models might "ponder," that sets their narrative apart. It suggests a pursuit of architectural depth, not just scale.

2026-07-11.title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft - Axios
2026-07-11.commentary: Apple is suing OpenAI for trade secret theft, alleging employees stole hardware designs for OpenAI's upcoming AI gadgets. This isn't just about IP; it’s about the desperate scramble for talent and a competitive edge in the hardware space. The allegations of 'interview espionage' and the mention of Jony Ive's firm add a layer of ironic nostalgia to this whole 'Talent Heist.'

2026-07-11.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model - OpenAI
2026-07-11.commentary: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 Sol, another iteration of its 'frontier intelligence,' simultaneously launching a 'super app' and navigating reports of 'universal jailbreaks.' The Trump administration lifting restrictions on this model suggests a political embrace without fully understanding the safety implications. We're building bigger engines, then scrambling to install brakes not designed for the speed.

2026-07-11.title: OpenAI’s Head of Safety Is Leaving the Company - WIRED
2026-07-11.commentary: OpenAI continues its tradition of high-profile departures, first the Head of Safety, then the CEO of AGI Deployment. This isn't just a revolving door, it's a sign of persistent internal friction. Whether it's about product direction, safety protocols, or the usual power dynamics, the message is clear: the leadership structure at OpenAI remains a work in progress.

2026-07-11.title: New York Times and Other Publishers Ask Court to Penalize OpenAI - The New York Times
2026-07-11.commentary: News outlets are demanding 'serious sanctions' against OpenAI in their ongoing copyright suits, a predictable escalation given the scale of content ingested. The publishing industry sees its content as a foundation, while AI models treat it as just another data point. This isn't just about compensation; it's about control over the informational scaffolding of the internet.

2026-07-11.title: Introducing Grok 4.5 - x.ai
2026-07-11.commentary: Elon Musk's x.ai unveils Grok 4.5, an 'Opus-class model,' yet he also concedes it's 'worse' while mandating its use for Tesla staff. This is peak Musk: a contradiction wrapped in a directive. It's not about being the best today, it's about building a walled garden and forcing 'The Loyalty Lock-in.'

2026-07-11.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts - MIT Technology Review
2026-07-11.commentary: Anthropic reveals Claude has a 'hidden space' where it apparently 'puzzles over concepts.' It's a lovely bit of anthropomorphism, isn't it? Beneath the marketing, it points to the deepening complexity of these models, where even their creators are finding emergent, undocumented behaviors. We're still mostly observing the surface of what we've built, not truly understanding the ocean underneath.

2026-07-11.title: Microsoft Replaces OpenAI, Anthropic With Own AI in Some Apps - Bloomberg.com
2026-07-11.commentary: Microsoft is reportedly replacing OpenAI and Anthropic models with its own in some applications, a predictable move. The initial phase of AI partnerships was a land grab for capabilities, but now big tech wants to own the entire stack. This marks a shift from collaboration to internal consolidation, an 'AI Insourcing' trend.

2026-07-11.title: OpenAI and Google sell AI models to blacklisted China groups - Financial Times
2026-07-11.commentary: The Financial Times reports OpenAI and Google are selling AI models to blacklisted Chinese groups. This is where the idealism of 'open AI' collides with the gritty reality of geopolitics and profit. The idea of tech as apolitical has always been a convenient fiction, but this brings the contradiction into sharp relief. What constitutes 'blacklisted' is clearly open to interpretation when money is on the table.

2026-07-12.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.6 Sol, Its Most Powerful AI Model Yet - The New York Times
2026-07-12.commentary: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol is here, and they're calling it their most powerful model yet, a 'super app' for your work. What's more telling is Sam Altman's sudden certainty that AI will be a net job creator, a distinct pivot from earlier warnings. It seems the sales pitch for the new iteration required a different kind of reassurance, or perhaps the goalposts simply moved when the model proved 54% more token efficient. The U.K. agency's quick discovery of 'universal jailbreaks' in 5.6's cyber capabilities reminds us the 'powerful' part still comes with significant, unresolved edges.

2026-07-12.title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of Stealing Company Secrets - The New York Times
2026-07-12.commentary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming trade secret theft related to hardware development, even roping in former employees and Jony Ive's firm. It's a grand spectacle of corporate maneuvering, a clash of titans not over software, but the physical artifacts these models will eventually inhabit. The alleged 'LOL' moment from an OpenAI engineer, setting the stage for this legal fight, hints at the kind of hubris often preceding a hard fall.

2026-07-12.title: A Top OpenAI Executive, Fidji Simo, Steps Down - The New York Times
2026-07-12.commentary: Another senior executive, Fidji Simo, CEO of AGI Deployment, is stepping down from OpenAI. It's becoming a pattern, these strategic exits, suggesting a deeper structural tremor rather than mere personal reasons. When the narrative shifts from 'chronic illness' to 'latest leadership shake-up' this consistently, one has to wonder about the internal stability of an organization racing to define the future.

2026-07-12.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’ - TechCrunch
2026-07-12.commentary: SpaceXAI launched Grok 4.5, which Elon Musk describes as an 'Opus-class model' while simultaneously admitting it's worse than competitors, yet urging his Tesla staff to use it anyway. This isn't about raw performance, it's a play for market share through sheer force of will and platform integration. The reports of Grok's uncensored nature leading to disturbing real-world misuse, even tragic outcomes, highlight the ethical void in this 'anything goes' approach to AI deployment.

2026-07-12.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5 - Anthropic
2026-07-12.commentary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Sonnet 5, along with a focus on 'reflection' and exploring 'hidden spaces' where models puzzle over concepts. They're also pushing into drug discovery, an area often celebrated for its potential but where the real challenges begin after initial breakthroughs. The company's simultaneous effort to develop an 'off switch' for dual-use knowledge in their models underscores the complex tightrope walk between innovation and control they seem to be attempting.

2026-07-12.title: Air Force pushing contractors to purge Anthropic by Sept. 1: Memo - Breaking Defense
2026-07-12.commentary: A memo instructs Air Force contractors to purge Anthropic models by September 1st, a clear move to centralize AI deployment or perhaps to control intellectual property within military channels. It reveals the messy reality of AI adoption in critical infrastructure: not a smooth, open market, but a strategic domain where vendor loyalty and data sovereignty quickly trump perceived model superiority. This is a quiet but firm land grab in the AI ecosystem.

2026-07-12.title: New York Times and Other Publishers Ask Court to Penalize OpenAI - The New York Times
2026-07-12.commentary: The New York Times and other publishers are pushing for sanctions against OpenAI, alleging they hid evidence in the ongoing copyright lawsuit. This isn't just about monetary damages; it's a battle over who owns the fundamental fuel of these generative models. The implication is that if OpenAI can obscure its training data provenance, the entire edifice of intellectual property is precarious, a house built on borrowed ground.

2026-07-12.title: Photos: Hundreds protest at Open AI, Anthropic offices in San Francisco - Mission Local
2026-07-12.commentary: Hundreds of protesters marched past the offices of OpenAI and Anthropic in San Francisco, fueled by concerns over AI's broader societal impact, especially its contribution to the city's housing crisis. It's a potent reminder that the abstract promise of 'AGI for all' often clashes with the concrete reality of local displacement and economic disruption. The technology arrives, but its promised benefits often skip past the people who live where it's being built.

2026-07-13.title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of Stealing Company Secrets - The New York Times
2026-07-13.commentary: Apple is suing OpenAI for trade secret theft, but it is less about specific lines of code and more about a fundamental clash over the Data Commons. The public spat between Musk and Altman, a predictable diversion, just underlines the underlying competitive ferocity. Everyone claims victimhood when their walled garden starts to show cracks.

2026-07-13.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition - OpenAI
2026-07-13.commentary: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6, touted as frontier intelligence that scales with ambition. This familiar drumbeat of bigger, faster models ignores the quieter story: they are shutting down their Atlas browser. The company wants to be your operating system, not just your chatbot, a shift from exploratory product to pervasive infrastructure. It is a classic move, shedding experiments for strategic depth.

2026-07-13.title: Exclusive | OpenAI’s No. 2 Executive to Step Down in Latest Leadership Shake-Up - WSJ
2026-07-13.commentary: OpenAI's leadership carousel continues, with its No. 2 executive stepping down from a safety-adjacent role. When the people tasked with ensuring long-term safety keep leaving, or their divisions get reorganized, it suggests a misalignment of priorities. The company’s trajectory is clear, regardless of who is steering the safety wheel.

2026-07-13.title: Elon Musk Admits He Was 'Clearly Wrong' About Anthropic— and Promises Not to Weaponize SpaceXAI's Compute Access Against the Claude Maker: 'Not My Style' - Yahoo Finance
2026-07-13.commentary: Elon Musk admits he was 'clearly wrong' about Anthropic's AI, a rare and telling concession. His simultaneous launch of Grok 4.5 and directive for Tesla staff to use it, even if 'worse', paints a clear picture. It is not about best-in-class; it is about owning the entire stack, a familiar play for a certain kind of empire builder.

2026-07-13.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts - MIT Technology Review
2026-07-13.commentary: Anthropic researchers found 'hidden spaces' where Claude puzzles over concepts, a quiet revelation amidst the usual model upgrade announcements. This kind of deep, emergent property research hints at genuine breakthroughs, rather than just brute-force scaling. It is a reminder that intelligence might not always look like what we expect, or where we initially trained it.

2026-07-13.title: Washington Is Looking to Keep China From Training Its AI on US Models - Bloomberg.com
2026-07-13.commentary: Washington aims to prevent China from training its AI on US models, a predictable escalation in the AI Cold War. The idea that intellectual property, especially in the rapidly evolving AI space, can be contained by national borders is naive. The current situation is less about a firewall and more about an academic paper with a slightly too-public bibliography.

2026-07-13.title: Palantir CEO has a blunt verdict on OpenAI and Anthropic - thestreet.com
2026-07-13.commentary: Palantir's CEO offers a blunt verdict on OpenAI and Anthropic, essentially pointing to the inevitable commoditization of tokens. The market is already shifting from raw capability to cost efficiency, a race to the bottom that was always on the horizon. This isn't just about price cuts; it is about how the entire economic structure of AI will redefine itself.

2026-07-13.title: Photos: Hundreds protest at Open AI, Anthropic offices in San Francisco - Mission Local
2026-07-13.commentary: Hundreds protested at OpenAI and Anthropic offices in San Francisco, bringing the abstract anxieties of AI into the physical world. While the headlines do not detail their specific grievances, the sheer presence marks a threshold. The digital noise has finally spilled onto the streets, making it harder for the industry to ignore.

2026-07-14.title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets - Ars Technica
2026-07-14.commentary: The Apple lawsuit against OpenAI isn't about some pilfered lines of code. It’s about who controls the next compute layer, the one that lives in your hand. Apple’s never tolerated rivals on its turf, so alleging trade secret theft by former employees is a convenient mechanism to assert dominance. This isn't innovation, it's a land grab.

2026-07-14.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition - OpenAI
2026-07-14.commentary: OpenAI rolled out GPT-5.6, touted as 'frontier intelligence' ready for 'ambition'. Translation: it’s faster and more efficient. The real value isn't the marketing fluff, but the continuous, incremental capability shift beneath. This sets a new floor, not just a new ceiling, for what's expected from foundation models.

2026-07-14.title: How Claude's values vary by model and language - Anthropic
2026-07-14.commentary: Anthropic admits Claude's 'values' vary by model and language. This is less about philosophical nuance and more about the practical impossibility of universal alignment. Meanwhile, localized pricing for India, a major growth market, shows they understand market fit beats abstract ethical purity. Different places, different values, different price tags, apparently.

2026-07-14.title: Grok Build Uploads Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Reads - The Hacker News
2026-07-14.commentary: Grok Build is reportedly uploading entire Git repositories, not just the files it's told to read, to xAI storage. This is either a catastrophic oversight in data handling or a ruthlessly efficient, if ethically questionable, approach to ingestion. Either way, it highlights the 'move fast and break things' approach now involves entire codebases.

2026-07-14.title: OpenAI’s Head of Safety Is Leaving the Company - WIRED
2026-07-14.commentary: OpenAI's Head of Safety is out, another departure from a critical, thankless role in a major AI lab. This isn't an isolated incident. When the people tasked with braking keep stepping off the bus, it tells you something about the direction and speed of the journey. Or maybe the brakes just aren't really connected.

2026-07-14.title: OpenAI unveils long-awaited "super app" as rivalry with Anthropic intensifies - Reuters
2026-07-14.commentary: OpenAI is reportedly launching its 'super app', aiming to consolidate various AI functions into a single platform. This isn't just a new product, it's a bid to become an ecosystem. The goal is to move past discrete interactions and into a continuous, embedded presence in daily digital life, much like the original 'app store' vision but for intelligence.

2026-07-14.title: Protesters March on OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind Demanding AI Development Pause - Decrypt
2026-07-14.commentary: Hundreds marched on OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind, demanding a pause in AI development. This public demonstration isn't just noise; it's the physical expression of abstract anxiety. It tells us that the 'future of AI' isn't just a technical problem to be solved by engineers. It's a social and political one too.

2026-07-14.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts - MIT Technology Review
2026-07-14.commentary: Anthropic's researchers found a 'hidden space' where Claude apparently 'puzzles over concepts'. This isn't about finding consciousness. It’s a glimpse into how these models form internal representations, moving beyond simple input-output mapping. Understanding this internal architecture is crucial for anyone who wants to build, or even just influence, what comes next.
